# glyphdust

> Scroll/agent-driven **text → particles → glyph → real-text resolve** for the web (WebGL/three.js).
> Also forms particles into any **SVG path shape** (icons, logos, symbols) via shape keyframes.
> You animate everything from a single progress `0 → 1`, through one entry point:
> the **`<GlyphDust>`** react-three-fiber component. MIT. npm package: `glyphdust`.

Designed for AI agents / codegen: minimal required config, safe defaults, and one `progress 0→1`
you can drive from anything — scroll, a timer, an AI agent, audio, sensor data. Copy an example
below and change the text; it works as-is.

Current version: 0.10.0. (The former React-free `glyphText()` one-call API and the CDN build
were removed in 0.10.0 — use 0.9.x if you need them.)

## Install

`npm i glyphdust three @react-three/fiber react react-dom`
(`three`, `@react-three/fiber`, `react`, `react-dom` are peerDependencies.)

## `<GlyphDust>` — React (react-three-fiber) component

Render it in your layout and pass `keyframes`. Scroll-driven by default. `resolveToDom` is a
**per-keyframe** flag (on the text keyframe), alongside `domSelector`.
```tsx
import { GlyphDust } from "glyphdust";
<GlyphDust
  keyframes={[
    { type: "text",    text: "Hello", domSelector: "#headline" },
    { type: "scatter", spread: 1 },
    { type: "text",    text: "WORLD", dense: true, resolveToDom: true },
  ]}
/>
```

`resolveToDom` works on **any** text keyframe with a `domSelector`, not just the last one:
while the journey stays on that keyframe, particles melt into the real DOM text
(crossfade + blur→focus), and melt back into particles when the journey moves on.
Consecutive keyframes with the same `domSelector` (form → hold) count as one stop.
The first stop starts as real text (visible from progress 0); the final stop stays as
real text. Do NOT drive those elements' opacity yourself — the library owns it.

## Props

- `keyframes: Keyframe[]` (required) — the timeline; typically `text → scatter → text`.
- `driver` — progress source (default `{ type: "scroll" }`):
  - `{ type: "scroll", triggerHeight? }` — full-screen sticky hero; `triggerHeight` ×100vh (default 2).
  - `{ type: "manual", progress }` — you supply `0..1` (scroll math, GSAP, an AI agent, anything).
  - `{ type: "autoplay", duration?, delay?, loop?, pingpong?, playOnView? }` — time-based; fills its parent box.
- `preset`: `"default" | "minimal" | "lively" | "glow"` · `style` — per-field overrides
  (`size`, `blend`, `drift`, `sparkle`, …). `style` always wins over `preset`.
- `colors`: `{ ink?, accent?, accentRatio? }` (defaults `#1b2330` / `#0055ff` / `0.18`).
- `count`: `{ desktop: 11000, mobile: 5200 }` · `timing`: `number[]` (normalized `0..1` per keyframe)
  · `camera`: `{ z: 7, fov: 42 }` · `dpr`: `[1, 1.75]`.
- `fallback: ReactNode` — rendered on reduced-motion / no-WebGL (never a blank screen).
- `resampleSignal: number` — change the value to re-sample `domSelector` targets while keeping
  the same WebGL context (for targets that drift from their initially-sampled position).
- `paused: boolean` — freeze the render loop without unmounting (WebGL context stays alive).
  Use for persistent multi-instance pools: keep every instance mounted, pause the idle ones.

## Keyframe types
- `{ type: "text", text, dense?, domSelector?, resolveToDom?, font?, segments?, worldW?, offsetX?, offsetY? }`
- `{ type: "scatter", spread? }`
- `{ type: "shape", path, viewBox?, fillRule?, worldW?, offsetX?, offsetY? }` — form particles
  into any **SVG path** (icon/logo/symbol). `path` = the `d` attribute (`string | string[]`,
  array = multi-`<path>` icon, all filled together). `viewBox` `[x,y,w,h]` optional
  (auto-measured); `fillRule` `"nonzero"|"evenodd"` (try `"evenodd"` if a hole gets filled);
  `worldW` = world width of the shape's bounding box (aspect preserved; tall shapes
  auto-shrink to stay visible when omitted). Shapes settle/hold like text glyphs but never
  resolve to DOM text.

Progress `0→1` auto-distributes across keyframes; a final text/shape keyframe forms by ~0.85 and holds.

## Gotchas
- **three.js / @react-three/fiber / react are required** — all peer dependencies.
- **`resolveToDom` needs a real DOM element per text keyframe** (`domSelector`). Without it the word
  stays as particles.
- **Accessibility / resilience**: `prefers-reduced-motion` or no-WebGL → renders `fallback`.

## Links
- README: https://github.com/linno-inc/glyphdust#readme
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/glyphdust
- Repo: https://github.com/linno-inc/glyphdust
